d2lr_e1_a.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_a.text: Yesterday's rough start has people talking — not in a good way. A group of customers are outside looking hesitant. Your reputation took a hit and it shows.

d2lr_e1_b.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_b.text: My friend said the service here was not great yesterday. We were thinking of going somewhere else. Is there any reason we should give you a chance?

choices.text: Apologize sincerely and offer a welcome discount
Acknowledge the issue and promise improvement
Defend yourself and explain what happened
Simply invite them in and let actions speak

d2lr_e1_c_apologize.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_c_apologize.text: Oh, that is actually really refreshing to hear. A discount and a genuine apology? We will give you another shot.

d2lr_e1_c_promise.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_c_promise.text: Okay, we appreciate the honesty. Everyone has bad days. We will come in and see.

d2lr_e1_c_defend.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_c_defend.text: Right. So it was the customer's fault? That is not a great sign. We think we will pass today.

d2lr_e1_c_invite.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_c_invite.text: Actions speak louder than words, that is true. Alright, let us see what you have got.

d2lr_e1_result_apologize.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_apologize.text: Genuine apologies with tangible gestures rebuild trust faster than words alone. You turned a skeptic into a potential regular.

d2lr_e1_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_promise.text: Acknowledging mistakes openly shows maturity. Now you must follow through — a promise without action makes things worse.

d2lr_e1_result_defend.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_defend.text: Defensiveness when your reputation is already low accelerates the damage. Customers want accountability not excuses.

d2lr_e1_result_invite.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_invite.text: Letting your service speak is powerful but risky when trust is already low. Make sure the experience delivers on that confidence.

d2lr_e2_a.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e2_a.text: Your regular supplier heard about your rough start and is being difficult about your next order, demanding payment upfront instead of the usual credit terms.

d2lr_e2_b.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e2_b.text: Given recent reports about your shop I am no longer comfortable with credit terms. Full payment upfront or no deal. What do you say?

choices.text: Pay upfront — maintain the supply chain
Explain your improvement plan to rebuild trust

d2lr_e2_c_pay.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e2_c_pay.text: Good. Payment received. Your order will be delivered tomorrow as usual. Keep up better performance and we can revisit the credit terms.

d2lr_e2_c_negotiate.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e2_c_negotiate.text: Half upfront is acceptable for now. Show me consistent improvement and we will return to normal terms within a month.

d2lr_e2_c_alternative.speaker: New Supplier
d2lr_e2_c_alternative.text: We can supply you but our rates are higher and quality is inconsistent until we establish a relationship. Starting from scratch is never easy.

d2lr_e2_c_explain.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e2_c_explain.text: I appreciate you coming to me directly with a plan. That shows character. Let us keep normal terms but I will be watching closely.

d2lr_e2_result_pay.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e2_result_pay.text: Maintaining supply chains even at cost is critical. A business without stock cannot serve customers no matter how good the recovery plan.

d2lr_e2_result_negotiate.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e2_result_negotiate.text: Negotiating middle ground preserves relationships while managing cash flow. Reputation damage creates real financial consequences — remember this.

d2lr_e2_result_alternative.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e2_result_alternative.text: Switching suppliers under pressure is risky. New relationships take time to build. Sometimes fixing existing relationships is smarter than starting over.

d2lr_e2_result_explain.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e2_result_explain.text: Transparency and a clear improvement plan can salvage damaged business relationships. People respect honesty and forward thinking over excuses.

d2lr_e3_a.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_a.text: End of a tough day. Your staff is feeling the pressure of the low reputation. Team morale is suffering and you need to address it before it gets worse.

d2lr_e3_b.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e3_b.text: Boss, the team is really down. People are saying negative things about our shop and it is affecting how we feel coming to work. What is the plan?

choices.text: Hold a team meeting and share a recovery plan
Acknowledge struggles and offer a team bonus
Remind them that pressure builds character
Focus on one small win to rebuild confidence

d2lr_e3_c_meeting.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e3_c_meeting.text: Thank you for being straight with us. Knowing there is a plan makes a huge difference. We are with you on this recovery.

d2lr_e3_c_bonus.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e3_c_bonus.text: A bonus really means a lot right now. It shows you appreciate us even when things are tough. We will push harder tomorrow.

d2lr_e3_c_pressure.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e3_c_pressure.text: Right... pressure builds character. That is easy to say when you are the boss. The team feels unsupported right now.

d2lr_e3_c_smallwin.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e3_c_smallwin.text: Yes! We got three five star compliments today. Focusing on those instead of the negatives really helps the team's perspective.

d2lr_e3_result_meeting.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_meeting.text: Transparent leadership during hard times builds lasting loyalty. Your team needs direction not just sympathy when things go wrong.

d2lr_e3_result_bonus.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_bonus.text: Financial recognition during hardship shows genuine care. It costs money now but buys loyalty that carries you through the recovery.

d2lr_e3_result_pressure.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_pressure.text: Dismissing team struggles with clichés damages trust further. When morale is already low your team needs empathy and direction not platitudes.

d2lr_e3_result_smallwin.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_smallwin.text: Celebrating small wins reframes adversity as progress. Good leaders find momentum in difficult moments rather than dwelling on setbacks.
